results.title: 7 meilleures applications pour apprendre l'anglais en 2026
excerpts:
Les applis sont parfaites pour démarrer, mais une formation t’apporte ce que les applis ne peuvent pas : méthode, correction, interaction humaine et pratique réelle.

Dans cet article, découvrez un comparatif des **7** **meilleures applications pour apprendre l’anglais en 2025** , pour que tu choisisses celle qui te correspond. Et pourquoi pas ensuite combiner l’une d’elles avec une bonne formation ?

## **Ce qu’il faut retenir**

* Les applications sont pratiques pour apprendre l’anglais à ton rythme, surtout en complément d’une formation.
* **Duolingo** et **Babbel** sont top pour les débutants, tandis que **HelloTalk** et **Busuu** permettent de parler avec de vrais anglophones.
* Si tu aimes les films et l’humour, **EWA English** est faite pour toi.
* Aucune appli n’est magique, mais toutes peuvent t’aider à créer une bonne habitude d’apprentissage.
* Pour parler couramment, pense aussi à une **formation d’anglais en ligne ou en présentiel** , avec un formateur natif.
## **Les meilleures applications mobiles pour apprendre l’anglais (2025)**

Que tu sois [débutant en anglais](https://www.myes.school/fr/magazine/conseils/apprendre-anglais-debutant/) , que tu veuilles simplement revoir les bases ou progresser rapidement, il existe une appli faite pour toi. Voici notre sélection des **7 meilleures applications pour apprendre l’anglais en 2025** .

## **1\. Duolingo : parfait pour débuter facilement et sans pression**

Duolingo est l’une des applications les plus populaires au monde pour apprendre une langue, et elle est idéale pour les débutants.

**Ce qu’on aime :**

* L’apprentissage se fait comme un jeu : tu gagnes des points, tu montes de niveau, et tu débloques des défis.

* Les leçons sont très courtes, colorées et faciles à suivre. On peut apprendre quelques minutes par jour sans se lasser.

**Ce qu’on aime moins :**

* Duolingo est moins efficace pour l’expression orale.
* Si tu as déjà un bon niveau, tu risques de t’ennuyer un peu car l’appli est surtout pensée pour débuter.

**Pour qui ?**

Débutants complets, enfants, ou adultes qui veulent apprendre en s’amusant.

## **2\. Babbel : une approche structurée pour les adultes motivés**

Babbel propose une méthode plus sérieuse, avec des leçons bien organisées et pensées pour les adultes.

**Ce qu’on aime :**

* Les cours suivent une progression logique. Chaque leçon aborde un point de grammaire, du vocabulaire, et des dialogues utiles.
* La prononciation est bien travaillée, avec des exercices d’écoute très précis.

**Ce qu’on aime moins :**

* Il faut payer dès les premières leçons pour débloquer le contenu.
* L’ambiance est un peu plus scolaire, ce qui peut être moins motivant pour certains.

**Pour qui ?**

Parfait pour les adultes qui veulent vraiment structurer leur apprentissage et progresser pas à pas.

## **3\. Busuu : idéal pour pratiquer avec de vrais anglophones**

**Duolingo, Inc.** <sup>[[ b ]]()</sup> is an American educational technology company that produces learning apps and provides language certification . Duolingo offers courses on 43 languages, <sup>[[ 5 ]](:3-7)</sup> ranging from English , French , and Spanish to less commonly studied languages such as Welsh , Irish , and Navajo , and even constructed languages such as Klingon . <sup>[[ 6 ]]()</sup> It also offers courses on music , <sup>[[ 7 ]]()</sup> math , and chess . <sup>[[ 8 ]]()</sup> The learning method incorporates gamification to motivate users with points, rewards and interactive lessons featuring spaced repetition . <sup>[[ 9 ]]()</sup> The app promotes short, daily lessons for consistent-phased practice.

Duolingo also offers the Duolingo English Test , an online language assessment , and Duolingo ABC, a literacy app designed for children. The company follows a freemium model, with optional premium services like Super Duolingo and Duolingo Max, which are ad-free and provide additional features. Additionally, Duolingo runs Duo's Taqueria , a Mexican taco restaurant in Pittsburgh.

With over 97.6 million million monthly active users, Duolingo is the most popular educational app in the world. <sup>[[ 10 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 11 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 12 ]]()</sup> <sup>[[ 13 ]]()</sup> Over 10 million people have a Duolingo streak longer than a year. <sup>[[ 14 ]]()</sup> In total, learners on Duolingo complete more than 13 billion exercises per week. <sup>[[ 15 ]]()</sup> A systematic review of research on Duolingo from 2012 to 2020 found comparatively few studies on the platform's efficiency for l

results.title: BBC Learning English - Find Out About Our App
excerpts:
**The BBC Learning English App (28 August 2018 – 13 December 2023)**

Hello and welcome to our page about our app. Unfortunately, the BBC Learning English app closed on 13 **<sup>th</sup>** December 2023. Since that date, the app has no longer worked, and you will not be able to access any of the programmes in the app or any programmes that you have downloaded.

**How to keep learning English with BBC Learning English**

Don’t worry, though. You still have many different ways that you can learn English with BBC Learning English and with your favourite BBC Learning English presenters.

**OUR WEBSITE**

Obviously, you can study here with us on our website. But you know that, right? You are here on our website right now! You’ll continue to be able to watch and listen to your old favourites like 6 Minute English and News Review. But, did you know, we also have a whole range of new materials being created for you for launch in early 2024?

**New content for 2024 (secret announcements!!!
)**

Look out for our new Business English series, **[Office English](https://www.bbc.co.uk/learningenglish/english/features/office-english)** , on the language you need to communicate effectively in the office. Check out our super-short, super-fun videos, [**Tenses With Georgie**](https://www.bbc.co.uk/learningenglish/english/features/tenses_with_georgie/) , that help you understand and improve your grammar. We’re also launching a new series to answer your questions about English. We also have a beginner-level 10-part drama and a series of short videos teaching 200 important words for learners with little or no English. But that’s not all, later in the year we will also be launching our new series to help you learn English through songs.

And all of that will be available first on our website 😊!

**SOCIAL MEDIA**

If you’re a fan of social media - and who isn’t? - we have many other ways for you to learn, share and love English with us. Find us on:

[YouTube](https://www.
youtube.com/bbclearningenglish)

[Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/bbclearningenglish.multimedia/)

[Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/bbclearningenglish/)

[TikTok](https://www.tiktok.com/@bbclearningenglish)

**PODCASTS**

Wherever you go, take us with you with our [podcasts](https://www.bbc.co.uk/learningenglish/english/podcasts) ; we'll always be there to help you with a quick English lesson.

[6 Minute English](https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p02pc9tn)

[The English We Speak](https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p02pc9zn/episodes/downloads)

[6 Minute Grammar](https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p02pc9wq/episodes/player)

[6 Minute Vocabulary](https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p02pc9xz/episodes/player)

[News Review](https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p05hw4bq/episodes/downloads)

[English in a Minute](https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p0bz8zmf/episodes/downloads)

[Learn English through drama](https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/p02pc9s1/episodes/downloads)

# About VoiceTube

**Welcome to the VoiceTube Blog!**

Here at the blog you can get weekly articles about language learning that will help you improve your reading skills while also solving some of the most common questions our fellow VoiceTubers have.

On the [English Learning Tips](https://www.blog.voicetube.com/category/english-learning-tips/) tab, you’ll find some of the most useful learning advice on the web! And on the [Business English](https://www.blog.voicetube.com/category/business-english/) tab, you’ll find work-oriented tips to help you navigate the professional world in English!

Got a question for us?
Send us an email at [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection) !

**About VoiceTube: Press Play and Learn!**

VoiceTube is an international English learning platform where you can learn English the practical way: watching all your favorite YouTube videos with subtitles! We have over 40,000 captioned videos drawn from YouTube, including TED talks, movie clips and trailers, TV series, music videos, work and business advice, BBC and CNN news pieces and other interesting videos covering a wide variety of topics.

Available on the [Web](http://www.voicetube.com) , [Android](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=org.redidea.voicetube&hl=en) and [iOS](https://itunes.apple.com/tw/app/voicetube-watch-videos-learn/id641446200?mt=8) , VoiceTube lets you learn English anytime, anywhere. So what are you waiting for? Just press play and learn!

## About this app

_arrow\_forward_

🗣️ Learn English in Minutes – Your Daily Dose of Real English!  
Looking for a fun, effective, and time-saving way to improve your English? Our app brings together three world-renowned programs to help you master English effortlessly:  
\- 🎧 6 Minutes – Explore everyday topics in culture, science, and lifestyle in just six minutes.
\- 💬 TEWS – Discover idioms, slang, and phrases used in real-life conversations.  
\- 🪶 Easy English – Learn simple vocabulary and basic sentence structures, perfect for beginners.  
  
🌟 Key Features:  
\- Short & engaging lessons: Each episode lasts just 3–6 minutes, ideal for busy learners.  
\- Bilingual subtitles: English and Vietnamese subtitles help you follow along and understand better.  
\- Practice listening, speaking & vocabulary: Boost your communication skills with interactive exercises.  
\- Offline access: Download lessons and learn anytime, anywhere.  
\- Weekly updates: Fresh content from BBC Learning English and trusted sources.  
  
🎯 Who is this app for?  
\- Beginners starting their English journey  
\- Busy professionals who want quick lessons  
\- Learners who love real-world English  
\- Students and workers aiming to improve listening and speaking skills  
  
🚀 Start Learning Today!
Whether you have just a few minutes a day or want to dive deep into English, this app is your perfect companion.

[Book Now](https://www.oxfordinternationalenglish.com/booking-form/booking-gateway/) [Enquire Now](https://www.oxfordinternationalenglish.com/enquiry-form-gateway/)

# What are the best apps for learning English?

Share

[Tweet](https://twitter.com/home?status=https://www.oxfordinternationalenglish.com/what-are-the-best-apps-for-learning-english/) [Share](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.oxfordinternationalenglish.com/what-are-the-best-apps-for-learning-english/) Email
When learning English, whether you happen to be in an English speaking country or not, you want to use as many tools as you can to get towards your target. There are a myriad of apps out there that can help you learn as you go, all based on your level and your needs.

Language learning apps are also a great way to gauge your level of reading and listening in preparation for a test such as the [Oxford ELLT](https://oidigitalinstitute.com/courses/higher-education/english-language-test/) .

**1\. Duolingo:** Possibly one of the most popular apps out there, [Duolingo](https://www.duolingo.com/) comes highly recommended for beginners. With just 20 minutes every day, you can learn English little by little by playing short games. Based on different topics, you can learn up to seven new words per topic and you gain skill points after completion of lessons.

**2\.
Babbel:** Another very popular language app, and for good reason – it is very effective if you want to acquire basic conversational skills. The focus of [Babbel](https://uk.babbel.com/) is vocabulary, so you get to understand the language better, rather than just memorising words. The lessons are divided in real-world topics, which means it ensures that you can start having comprehensible conversations sooner.

**3\. [FluentU](https://www.fluentu.com/en/) :** This app is quite unique, as it uses engaging real-world videos; such as commercials and news, to turn them into English-learning experiences. You ease into a natural culture and similar to Babbel, you can start having conversation right away on every day or more meaningful topics. Learning English as it’s spoken in real life will also help you form an accent, if that’s what you are also after.

**4\. Rosetta Stone:** The most famous app, the method it uses is also quite a unique one, because [Rosetta Stone](https://uk.

• Duolingo works. Designed by learning experts, Duolingo has a science-based teaching methodology proven to foster long-term knowledge retention.  
  
• Track your progress. Work toward your learning goals with playful rewards and achievements when you make practicing a daily habit!  
  
• Join millions of learners. Stay motivated with competitive Leaderboards as you learn alongside our global community.

Learn Spanish, French, German, Italian, Russian, Portuguese, Turkish, Dutch, Irish, Danish, Swedish, Ukrainian, Esperanto, Polish, Greek, Hungarian, Norwegian, Hebrew, Welsh, Arabic, Latin, Hawaiian, Scottish Gaelic, Vietnamese, Korean, Japanese, English, and even High Valyrian! results.title: The world’s best way to improve your English ... - ELSA Speak
excerpts: ELSA, English Language Speech Assistant, is a fun and engaging app specially designed to help you improve your English-speaking communication skills. ELSA's artificial intelligence technology was developed using voice data of people speaking English with various accents.
